== {{神社 |名称 = Okataka Shrine |画像 = [[Image:Okataka-jinjya.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}} Kitanogō-chō 292 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|26|11.44|N|136|19|56.30|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |創建 = Trad. {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=459|Q30500|WD=Q30500|1=459}}) |社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |例祭 = Saturday/Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531|1=April 25}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Okataka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Okataka Shrine''' (おかたかじんじゃ), official name: Okataka Shrine, is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in Kitanogō-chō, {{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. ===Enshrined deities=== It enshrines {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} (すがわらみちざねこう) and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. ===Shrine crest=== Star-plum-bowl crest ===History=== In year 3 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} ({{ill|lt=459|Q30500|WD=Q30500|1=459}}), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gamō no Inagi Samamaro|1=UNKNOWN}} first enshrined {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} on Senjōo-dake, 2 km east of the current site; in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 13 ({{ill|lt=685|Q33480|WD=Q33480|1=685}}), it was moved to the current otabisho. In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), {{ill|lt=Ono no Takamura|Q1139928|WD=Q1139928|1=Ono no Takamura}} built shrine buildings at the current site and called it Okamoto Shrine. At that time this area was Okamoto-gō of Asai District. During syncretic times, there was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okatakayama Jingū-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the Sengoku period, clergy avoided {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}’s temple-shrine burnings by inviting from Kyoto’s {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|WD=Q662176|1=Kitano Tenmangū}}, having heard that his father Nobuhide revered {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}}, and called it Okataka Tenjin. From early Meiji it became Okataka Shrine; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}) it became {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and in Meiji 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819|1=1884}}) it became {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. As the sōsha of 15 villages of Shimokusano-shō, it was deeply revered. In droughts, rain prayers were held, choosing among taiko dance, yuno-hana, and tōmyō rites; when prayers were fulfilled, a thanksgiving taiko dance by all 15 villages was dedicated. This rain-prayer custom continued until before the war; now it has ceased, with three taiko dance preservation groups remaining. The main sanctuary was built in {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}}) by Ubahuchi Jirosuke (Kaodo Village) as nagare-zukuri with chidori-karahafu. The worship hall was completed in Meiji 21 by master builder {{ill|lt=Itō Heizaemon IX|Q17193360|WD=Q17193360|lt=Itō Heizaemon Morimichi|1=Itō Heizaemon IX}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}} as an irimoyao-zukuri, cypress-bark roof, 4-ken frontage by 3-ken depth. The middle gate and corridor fence were built in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|WD=Q18787|1=1930}}), and the shrine office was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062|1=1991}}) as an imperial enthronement commemorative project. === Festivals === * Annual festival: Saturday/Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531|1=April 25}} * Tōmyō Festival: {{ill|lt=August 25|Q2819|WD=Q2819|1=August 25}} === Shrine treasures === * Komainu (wooden) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1077 (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:長浜市の建築物]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), {{ill|lt=Ono no Takamura|Q1139928|WD=Q1139928|1=Ono no Takamura}} built shrine buildings at the current site and called it Okamoto Shrine. At that time this area was Okamoto-gō of Asai District. During syncretic times, there was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okatakayama Jingū-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the Sengoku period, clergy avoided {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}’s temple-shrine burnings by inviting from Kyoto’s {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|WD=Q662176|1=Kitano Tenmangū}}, having heard that his father Nobuhide revered {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}}, and called it Okataka Tenjin. From early Meiji it became Okataka Shrine; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}) it became {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and in Meiji 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819|1=1884}}) it became {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. As the sōsha of 15 villages of Shimokusano-shō, it was deeply revered. In droughts, rain prayers were held, choosing among taiko dance, yuno-hana, and tōmyō rites; when prayers were fulfilled, a thanksgiving taiko dance by all 15 villages was dedicated. This rain-prayer custom continued until before the war; now it has ceased, with three taiko dance preservation groups remaining. The main sanctuary was built in {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}}) by Ubahuchi Jirosuke (Kaodo Village) as nagare-zukuri with chidori-karahafu. The worship hall was completed in Meiji 21 by master builder {{ill|lt=Itō Heizaemon IX|Q17193360|WD=Q17193360|lt=Itō Heizaemon Morimichi|1=Itō Heizaemon IX}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}} as an irimoyao-zukuri, cypress-bark roof, 4-ken frontage by 3-ken depth. The middle gate and corridor fence were built in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|WD=Q18787|1=1930}}), and the shrine office was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062|1=1991}}) as an imperial enthronement commemorative project. === Festivals === * Annual festival: Saturday/Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531|1=April 25}} * Tōmyō Festival: {{ill|lt=August 25|Q2819|WD=Q2819|1=August 25}} === Shrine treasures === * Komainu (wooden) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1077 (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:長浜市の建築物]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== {{神社 |名称 = Okataka Shrine |画像 = [[Image:Okataka-jinjya.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}} Kitanogō-chō 292 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|26|11.44|N|136|19|56.30|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |創建 = Trad. {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=459|Q30500|WD=Q30500|1=459}}) |社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |例祭 = Saturday/Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531|1=April 25}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Okataka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Okataka Shrine''' (おかたかじんじゃ), official name: Okataka Shrine, is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in Kitanogō-chō, {{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. ===Enshrined deities=== It enshrines {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} (すがわらみちざねこう) and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. ===Shrine crest=== Star-plum-bowl crest ===History=== In year 3 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} ({{ill|lt=459|Q30500|WD=Q30500|1=459}}), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gamō no Inagi Samamaro|1=UNKNOWN}} first enshrined {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} on Senjōo-dake, 2 km east of the current site; in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 13 ({{ill|lt=685|Q33480|WD=Q33480|1=685}}), it was moved to the current otabisho. In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), {{ill|lt=Ono no Takamura|Q1139928|WD=Q1139928|1=Ono no Takamura}} built shrine buildings at the current site and called it Okamoto Shrine. At that time this area was Okamoto-gō of Asai District. During syncretic times, there was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okatakayama Jingū-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the Sengoku period, clergy avoided {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}’s temple-shrine burnings by inviting from Kyoto’s {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|WD=Q662176|1=Kitano Tenmangū}}, having heard that his father Nobuhide revered {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}}, and called it Okataka Tenjin. From early Meiji it became Okataka Shrine; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}) it became {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and in Meiji 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819|1=1884}}) it became {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. As the sōsha of 15 villages of Shimokusano-shō, it was deeply revered. In droughts, rain prayers were held, choosing among taiko dance, yuno-hana, and tōmyō rites; when prayers were fulfilled, a thanksgiving taiko dance by all 15 villages was dedicated. This rain-prayer custom continued until before the war; now it has ceased, with three taiko dance preservation groups remaining. The main sanctuary was built in {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}}) by Ubahuchi Jirosuke (Kaodo Village) as nagare-zukuri with chidori-karahafu. The worship hall was completed in Meiji 21 by master builder {{ill|lt=Itō Heizaemon IX|Q17193360|WD=Q17193360|lt=Itō Heizaemon Morimichi|1=Itō Heizaemon IX}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}} as an irimoyao-zukuri, cypress-bark roof, 4-ken frontage by 3-ken depth. The middle gate and corridor fence were built in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|WD=Q18787|1=1930}}), and the shrine office was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062|1=1991}}) as an imperial enthronement commemorative project. === Festivals === * Annual festival: Saturday/Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531|1=April 25}} * Tōmyō Festival: {{ill|lt=August 25|Q2819|WD=Q2819|1=August 25}} === Shrine treasures === * Komainu (wooden) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1077 (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:長浜市の建築物]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== {{神社 |名称 = Okataka Shrine |画像 = [[Image:Okataka-jinjya.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}} Kitanogō-chō 292 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|26|11.44|N|136|19|56.30|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |創建 = Trad. {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=459|Q30500|WD=Q30500|1=459}}) |社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |例祭 = Saturday/Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531|1=April 25}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Okataka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Okataka Shrine''' (おかたかじんじゃ), official name: Okataka Shrine, is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in Kitanogō-chō, {{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. ===Enshrined deities=== It enshrines {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} (すがわらみちざねこう) and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. ===Shrine crest=== Star-plum-bowl crest ===History=== In year 3 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} ({{ill|lt=459|Q30500|WD=Q30500|1=459}}), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gamō no Inagi Samamaro|1=UNKNOWN}} first enshrined {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} on Senjōo-dake, 2 km east of the current site; in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 13 ({{ill|lt=685|Q33480|WD=Q33480|1=685}}), it was moved to the current otabisho. In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), {{ill|lt=Ono no Takamura|Q1139928|WD=Q1139928|1=Ono no Takamura}} built shrine buildings at the current site and called it Okamoto Shrine. At that time this area was Okamoto-gō of Asai District. During syncretic times, there was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okatakayama Jingū-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the Sengoku period, clergy avoided {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}’s temple-shrine burnings by inviting from Kyoto’s {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|WD=Q662176|1=Kitano Tenmangū}}, having heard that his father Nobuhide revered {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}}, and called it Okataka Tenjin. From early Meiji it became Okataka Shrine; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}) it became {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and in Meiji 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819|1=1884}}) it became {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. As the sōsha of 15 villages of Shimokusano-shō, it was deeply revered. In droughts, rain prayers were held, choosing among taiko dance, yuno-hana, and tōmyō rites; when prayers were fulfilled, a thanksgiving taiko dance by all 15 villages was dedicated. This rain-prayer custom continued until before the war; now it has ceased, with three taiko dance preservation groups remaining. The main sanctuary was built in {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}}) by Ubahuchi Jirosuke (Kaodo Village) as nagare-zukuri with chidori-karahafu. The worship hall was completed in Meiji 21 by master builder {{ill|lt=Itō Heizaemon IX|Q17193360|WD=Q17193360|lt=Itō Heizaemon Morimichi|1=Itō Heizaemon IX}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}} as an irimoyao-zukuri, cypress-bark roof, 4-ken frontage by 3-ken depth. The middle gate and corridor fence were built in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|WD=Q18787|1=1930}}), and the shrine office was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062|1=1991}}) as an imperial enthronement commemorative project. === Festivals === * Annual festival: Saturday/Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531|1=April 25}} * Tōmyō Festival: {{ill|lt=August 25|Q2819|WD=Q2819|1=August 25}} === Shrine treasures === * Komainu (wooden) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1077 (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagahama, Shiga]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), {{ill|lt=Ono no Takamura|Q1139928|WD=Q1139928|1=Ono no Takamura}} built shrine buildings at the current site and called it Okamoto Shrine. At that time this area was Okamoto-gō of Asai District. During syncretic times, there was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okatakayama Jingū-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the Sengoku period, clergy avoided {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}’s temple-shrine burnings by inviting from Kyoto’s {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|WD=Q662176|1=Kitano Tenmangū}}, having heard that his father Nobuhide revered {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}}, and called it Okataka Tenjin. From early Meiji it became Okataka Shrine; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}) it became {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and in Meiji 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819|1=1884}}) it became {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. As the sōsha of 15 villages of Shimokusano-shō, it was deeply revered. In droughts, rain prayers were held, choosing among taiko dance, yuno-hana, and tōmyō rites; when prayers were fulfilled, a thanksgiving taiko dance by all 15 villages was dedicated. This rain-prayer custom continued until before the war; now it has ceased, with three taiko dance preservation groups remaining. The main sanctuary was built in {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}}) by Ubahuchi Jirosuke (Kaodo Village) as nagare-zukuri with chidori-karahafu. The worship hall was completed in Meiji 21 by master builder {{ill|lt=Itō Heizaemon IX|Q17193360|WD=Q17193360|lt=Itō Heizaemon Morimichi|1=Itō Heizaemon IX}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}} as an irimoyao-zukuri, cypress-bark roof, 4-ken frontage by 3-ken depth. The middle gate and corridor fence were built in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|WD=Q18787|1=1930}}), and the shrine office was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062|1=1991}}) as an imperial enthronement commemorative project. === Festivals === * Annual festival: Saturday/Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531|1=April 25}} * Tōmyō Festival: {{ill|lt=August 25|Q2819|WD=Q2819|1=August 25}} === Shrine treasures === * Komainu (wooden) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1077 (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagahama, Shiga]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:pages]]

== {{神社 |名称 = Okataka Shrine |画像 = [[Image:Okataka-jinjya.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}} Kitanogō-chō 292 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|26|11.44|N|136|19|56.30|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |創建 = Trad. {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=459|Q30500|WD=Q30500|1=459}}) |社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |例祭 = Saturday/Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531|1=April 25}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Okataka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Okataka Shrine''' (おかたかじんじゃ), official name: Okataka Shrine, is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in Kitanogō-chō, {{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. ===Enshrined deities=== It enshrines {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} (すがわらみちざねこう) and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. ===Shrine crest=== Star-plum-bowl crest ===History=== In year 3 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} ({{ill|lt=459|Q30500|WD=Q30500|1=459}}), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gamō no Inagi Samamaro|1=UNKNOWN}} first enshrined {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} on Senjōo-dake, 2 km east of the current site; in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 13 ({{ill|lt=685|Q33480|WD=Q33480|1=685}}), it was moved to the current otabisho. In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), {{ill|lt=Ono no Takamura|Q1139928|WD=Q1139928|1=Ono no Takamura}} built shrine buildings at the current site and called it Okamoto Shrine. At that time this area was Okamoto-gō of Asai District. During syncretic times, there was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okatakayama Jingū-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the Sengoku period, clergy avoided {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}’s temple-shrine burnings by inviting from Kyoto’s {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|WD=Q662176|1=Kitano Tenmangū}}, having heard that his father Nobuhide revered {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}}, and called it Okataka Tenjin. From early Meiji it became Okataka Shrine; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}) it became {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and in Meiji 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819|1=1884}}) it became {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. As the sōsha of 15 villages of Shimokusano-shō, it was deeply revered. In droughts, rain prayers were held, choosing among taiko dance, yuno-hana, and tōmyō rites; when prayers were fulfilled, a thanksgiving taiko dance by all 15 villages was dedicated. This rain-prayer custom continued until before the war; now it has ceased, with three taiko dance preservation groups remaining. The main sanctuary was built in {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}}) by Ubahuchi Jirosuke (Kaodo Village) as nagare-zukuri with chidori-karahafu. The worship hall was completed in Meiji 21 by master builder {{ill|lt=Itō Heizaemon IX|Q17193360|WD=Q17193360|lt=Itō Heizaemon Morimichi|1=Itō Heizaemon IX}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}} as an irimoyao-zukuri, cypress-bark roof, 4-ken frontage by 3-ken depth. The middle gate and corridor fence were built in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|WD=Q18787|1=1930}}), and the shrine office was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062|1=1991}}) as an imperial enthronement commemorative project. === Festivals === * Annual festival: Saturday/Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531|1=April 25}} * Tōmyō Festival: {{ill|lt=August 25|Q2819|WD=Q2819|1=August 25}} === Shrine treasures === * Komainu (wooden) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1077 (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagahama, Shiga]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}} Kitanogō-chō 292 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|26|11.44|N|136|19|56.30|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |創建 = Trad. {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=459|Q30500|WD=Q30500|1=459}}) |社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, candidate shrine for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |例祭 = Saturday/Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531|1=April 25}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Okataka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Okataka Shrine''' (おかたかじんじゃ), official name: Okataka Shrine, is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in Kitanogō-chō, {{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. ===Enshrined deities=== It enshrines {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} (すがわらみちざねこう) and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. ===Shrine crest=== Star-plum-bowl crest ===History=== In year 3 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|WD=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} ({{ill|lt=459|Q30500|WD=Q30500|1=459}}), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gamō no Inagi Samamaro|1=UNKNOWN}} first enshrined {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} on Senjōo-dake, 2 km east of the current site; in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 13 ({{ill|lt=685|Q33480|WD=Q33480|1=685}}), it was moved to the current otabisho. In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), {{ill|lt=Ono no Takamura|Q1139928|WD=Q1139928|1=Ono no Takamura}} built shrine buildings at the current site and called it Okamoto Shrine. At that time this area was Okamoto-gō of Asai District. During syncretic times, there was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okatakayama Jingū-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the Sengoku period, clergy avoided {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}’s temple-shrine burnings by inviting from Kyoto’s {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|WD=Q662176|1=Kitano Tenmangū}}, having heard that his father Nobuhide revered {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}}, and called it Okataka Tenjin. From early Meiji it became Okataka Shrine; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}) it became {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and in Meiji 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819|1=1884}}) it became {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. As the sōsha of 15 villages of Shimokusano-shō, it was deeply revered. In droughts, rain prayers were held, choosing among taiko dance, yuno-hana, and tōmyō rites; when prayers were fulfilled, a thanksgiving taiko dance by all 15 villages was dedicated. This rain-prayer custom continued until before the war; now it has ceased, with three taiko dance preservation groups remaining. The main sanctuary was built in {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}}) by Ubahuchi Jirosuke (Kaodo Village) as nagare-zukuri with chidori-karahafu. The worship hall was completed in Meiji 21 by master builder {{ill|lt=Itō Heizaemon IX|Q17193360|WD=Q17193360|lt=Itō Heizaemon Morimichi|1=Itō Heizaemon IX}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}} as an irimoyao-zukuri, cypress-bark roof, 4-ken frontage by 3-ken depth. The middle gate and corridor fence were built in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|WD=Q18787|1=1930}}), and the shrine office was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062|1=1991}}) as an imperial enthronement commemorative project. === Festivals === * Annual festival: Saturday/Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531|1=April 25}} * Tōmyō Festival: {{ill|lt=August 25|Q2819|WD=Q2819|1=August 25}} === Shrine treasures === * Komainu (wooden) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1077 (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagahama, Shiga]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]]

In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}}), {{ill|lt=Ono no Takamura|Q1139928|WD=Q1139928|1=Ono no Takamura}} built shrine buildings at the current site and called it Okamoto Shrine. At that time this area was Okamoto-gō of Asai District. During syncretic times, there was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okatakayama Jingū-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}. From early Meiji it became Okataka Shrine; in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}) it became {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and in Meiji 17 ({{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|WD=Q7819|1=1884}}) it became {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. As the sōsha of 15 villages of Shimokusano-shō, it was deeply revered. In droughts, rain prayers were held, choosing among taiko dance, yuno-hana, and tōmyō rites; when prayers were fulfilled, a thanksgiving taiko dance by all 15 villages was dedicated. This rain-prayer custom continued until before the war; now it has ceased, with three taiko dance preservation groups remaining. The main sanctuary was built in {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}}) by Ubahuchi Jirosuke (Kaodo Village) as nagare-zukuri with chidori-karahafu. The worship hall was completed in Meiji 21 by master builder {{ill|lt=Itō Heizaemon IX|Q17193360|WD=Q17193360|lt=Itō Heizaemon Morimichi|1=Itō Heizaemon IX}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}} as an irimoyao-zukuri, cypress-bark roof, 4-ken frontage by 3-ken depth. The middle gate and corridor fence were built in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|WD=Q18787|1=1930}}), and the shrine office was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062|1=1991}}) as an imperial enthronement commemorative project. === Festivals === * Annual festival: Saturday/Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531|1=April 25}} * Tōmyō Festival: {{ill|lt=August 25|Q2819|WD=Q2819|1=August 25}} === Shrine treasures === * Komainu (wooden) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1077 (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okataka Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagahama, Shiga]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]]
